title: T-120 — Restore drill runner (tooling/restore-drill/) — logical restore + smoke-query set
prompt:
# T-120 — Restore drill runner

PRD: docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md §13 Niefunkcjonalne ("DR documented + tested quarterly")
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Provide a script in `tooling/restore-drill/` that performs a **logical restore** of the most-recent backup dump against an ephemeral Postgres database and runs a smoke-query set asserting (a) the schema migrated to current head, (b) every business table has ≥1 row from the restored dump, (c) `app.current_org_id()` function is callable, (d) RLS policies exist for the placeholder R13 tables (T-040).

This closes the §13 "tested quarterly" half of the DR criterion — T-119 documents the policy + verifies daily backup freshness; T-120 actually exercises a restore.

## Implementation contract
1. Create `tooling/restore-drill/` directory with:
   - `package.json` (private, scripts: `drill`, `test`).
   - `src/run-drill.ts` — entrypoint that:
     a. Reads `DRILL_DUMP_PATH` (path to a .sql or .dump file, fetched out-of-band from S3 / Supabase) and `DRILL_DATABASE_URL` (ephemeral target, e.g. testcontainers Postgres or a fresh Supabase branch).
     b. Restores the dump via `pg_restore` or `psql -f` shell-out.
     c. Runs `pnpm --filter @monopilot/db migrate` against the restored DB to bring schema to head.
     d. Executes the smoke-query suite from `src/smoke-queries.ts`.
     e. Emits a JSON report `{startedAt, restoredAt, migratedAt, smokeResults: [...], passed: boolean}`.
   - `src/smoke-queries.ts` — array of `{name, sql, assert}` entries:
     - `schema_version` — assert `SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'` > N (parametric).
     - `placeholder_r13_rls` — assert `pg_policies` has at least 5 policies on the T-040 placeholder tables.
     - `org_context_callable` — `SELECT app.current_org_id()` returns NULL (no context set) without error.
     - `audit_events_present` — `SELECT count(*) FROM audit_events` returns finite integer.
     - `outbox_events_table_present` — `SELECT 1 FROM outbox_events LIMIT 1` doesn't error.
   - `src/__tests__/run-drill.test.ts` — integration test stub that:
     - Spins up testcontainers Postgres,
     - Generates a minimal fixture dump via `pg_dump`,
     - Runs `run-drill` against the ephemeral target,
     - Asserts every smoke result `passed===true`.
2. Add `pnpm drill` script to root `package.json` that forwards to `pnpm --filter @monopilot/restore-drill drill`.
3. Document the quarterly drill expectation in `_foundation/contracts/backup-policy.md` (T-119) — append a section linking to this task and explaining how the drill is run (CI-scheduled quarterly via GitHub Actions; or operator manual `pnpm drill`).

## Files in scope
- tooling/restore-drill/package.json [create]
- tooling/restore-drill/tsconfig.json [create]
- tooling/restore-drill/src/run-drill.ts [create]
- tooling/restore-drill/src/smoke-queries.ts [create]
- tooling/restore-drill/src/__tests__/run-drill.test.ts [create]
- package.json [modify — add `drill` script]
- _foundation/contracts/backup-policy.md [modify — append quarterly drill section]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given minimal fixture dump (generated by pg_dump in test setup), when run-drill invoked against ephemeral testcontainers Postgres, then JSON report has passed:true and every smokeResults entry has passed:true.
2. Given dump is corrupt (truncated mid-statement), when run-drill invoked, then exits non-zero, JSON report has passed:false, failing phase named in report.
3. Given pnpm drill run at root, when script forwards to workspace, then invokes run-drill with current env.
4. Given smoke-queries file, when placeholder_r13_rls asserted, then pg_policies returns at least 5 rows where tablename IN ('lot','work_order','quality_event','shipment','bom_item') per T-040; AND Given _foundation/contracts/backup-policy.md read after this task, when 'Quarterly restore drill' section found, then links to tooling/restore-drill/ and references this task ID.
## Test strategy
- RED: write `run-drill.test.ts` with the 5 ACs (using testcontainers Postgres + fixture pg_dump) before implementation. RED: `pnpm --filter @monopilot/restore-drill test`.
- GREEN: implement run-drill + smoke-queries + script registration.
- Run: `pnpm --filter @monopilot/restore-drill test && pnpm drill --help` (latter to confirm script wiring).

## Risk red lines
- Do not run the drill against a production DB — `DRILL_DATABASE_URL` MUST be ephemeral; check `host` is not in a prod allowlist.
- Do not write the drill output JSON into the repo — write to `./drill-reports/` (gitignored).
- Do not commit any real backup dump — fixture only for tests.
- Do not skip the migrate step — a restored dump must be brought to schema head before smoke queries.
- Do not assume specific row counts — assertions are existence + structural, not value-equal.
- Do not invoke the drill from `apps/worker` or `apps/web` — it's an operator/CI tool only.

pipeline_inputs.description: Script in tooling/restore-drill/ that performs logical restore against ephemeral Postgres + runs smoke-query suite (schema, RLS, org-context, audit_events, outbox). Integration tests stub via testcontainers. Closes §13 'tested quarterly' DR criterion.
pipeline_inputs.details:
run-drill.ts reads DRILL_DUMP_PATH + DRILL_DATABASE_URL, restores via pg_restore, runs migrations, executes smoke-queries.ts checks, emits JSON report. Smoke set includes placeholder R13 RLS check (T-040 list), app.current_org_id() callable, audit_events + outbox_events tables present. Tests use fixture pg_dump in testcontainers Postgres. Quarterly cadence operator/CI-driven (gitHub Actions schedule cron preferred).

AC count consolidated from 5→4 by Fixer F7 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost).
out_of_scope: Quarterly drill GitHub Actions cron wiring (optional follow-up; CI task T-122 may add it)
Real production restore — fixture only here
